    [Comment 9] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 27 Jan 2026 15:26:00 +0100):

    SASS compiler

    Update to angular 19, brought sass@1.85.0 which marks usages of "@import" in scss files as deprecated. As we were using it extensively build- and browser consoles are spammed with warnings.

    Was able to migrate to new syntax in stylesheets of components, however not in global stylesheets, which are importing 3rd party stylesheets we have no control over (boostrap4, bnotk/ds-ng).

        Hi everyone,

        ticket got renamed to Angular 19.

        Filip Misiorek & Oleksii Samusenko your T&M efforts for analysis please still book on this ticket. I'll update the description of above's ticket soon to fit to this task, once confirmed by [Comment 14] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 13 Jan 2026 14:21:58 +0100):

        ag-grid has quite extensive list of breaking changes, accommodating them will take some effort:
        https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/upgrading-to-ag-grid-32/#breaking-changes
         
        Should be on UVZ:
        node.js v22.12.0
        "typescript": "5.9.0", (5.8.0 with Angular 19.2)
         
        From Angular documentation  RxJS should be set to ^6.5.3 or ^7.4.0, so we are fine with that because we are now using rxjs 7.4.0 version.
         
        This part is basing on:

implementation_steps: 1. Update Node.js version in .nvmrc / package.json to the version required by Angular 19 (e.g., >=20.x).
2. Upgrade TypeScript to the version compatible with Angular 19 (e.g., 5.4).
3. Modify package.json dependencies: replace "@angular/core": "18.x" with "@angular/core": "19.x" and update all Angular packages accordingly.
4. Update Pattern Library (PL) dependency to "@bnotk/ds-ng": "12.6.0" in package.json.

5. Align third‑party libraries to versions compatible with Angular 19: ng-bootstrap, ng-select, ng-option-highlight, ag-grid-angular, ag-grid-community (use the versions referenced in Filip Misiorek's analysis).
6. Apply the custom ng-bootstrap patch from UVZUSLNVV-5824 and run npm install to ensure the patched code is included.
7. Refactor any Angular 18 specific APIs (e.g., deprecated lifecycle hooks) in the front‑end source files; update imports in components that reference old PL 11.3.1 assets.
8. In IndexHTMLResourceService, adjust the served index.html to reference the new Angular bundle filenames and PL 12.6.0 CSS/JS assets.
9. Verify that the vertical action bar component still loads (it is deprecated but supported until PL 13.2.0); update its module imports if necessary.
10. Remove obsolete Angular 18 and PL 11.3.1 files from the repository (e.g., old polyfills, style sheets) after confirming they are not required by Angular 19/PL 12.6.0.
11. Run full build, fix compilation errors, and update CI pipeline to use the new Node.js and TypeScript versions.
12. Execute regression test suite and update failing tests to align with Angular 19 APIs.

unit_tests: Run existing unit tests for all Angular components after upgrade; add tests for any newly introduced PL 12.6.0 components.
Create unit tests for the vertical action bar to ensure it renders correctly with PL 12.6.0.
integration_tests: Execute integration tests for all REST endpoints listed in interfaces to verify that front‑end calls succeed with the upgraded bundles.
Perform end‑to‑end tests for critical user flows (e.g., deed entry creation, business purpose retrieval) using the upgraded UI.

security_considerations: Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (security pattern: cors in ActionRestService).
Verify CSRF protection is enabled for all state‑changing operations (security pattern: csrf in AbstractSecurityConfiguration).
Confirm pre_authorize annotations are present on sensitive services (security pattern: pre_authorize in KeyManagerRestService).
validation_strategy: Add @NotNull annotations to DeedEntry fields as recommended (validation pattern: not_null on DeedEntry).
Add @Valid annotations to DeedEntryRestService method parameters (validation pattern: valid on DeedEntryRestService).
Apply @NotBlank, @Min, and @Max annotations to KmConfig properties (validation patterns: not_blank, min, max on KmConfig).
error_handling: Create or reuse custom RuntimeException classes for domain errors (error pattern: RuntimeException custom_exception).
Add @ExceptionHandler methods for generic Exception, HttpClientErrorException, and HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException in a global exception handler (error patterns: exception_handler).

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The upgrade touches core front‑end framework, multiple third‑party libraries, and requires coordination with backend services for asset delivery, but the modular monolith structure limits impact to well‑defined components.
risks: Incompatible third‑party library versions causing runtime errors.
Deprecated vertical action bar may be removed in future PL releases, leading to hidden breakage.
Potential mismatches between backend served assets (IndexHTMLResourceService) and new bundle filenames.
